steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content:
The passage sets up a contrast: Seira et al. found that risk-disclosure messages had limited effects, yet they assert that such effects “may nevertheless be worth pursuing”. The word “given” directly connects the underlined portion (negligible cost) as the stated justification for this assertion.

This is the decisive clue to the underlined portion's function.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the choices
steps.content:
Choice A (‘It notes a factor that led Seira et al. to not dismiss risk-disclosure messaging altogether despite their evidence of its limited utility.’) correctly identifies that negligible cost is what prevents dismissal of the approach.

The other choices misidentify the function: B introduces a different topic, C confuses Seira et al.'s assertion with a recommendation, and D mistakes cost for effectiveness.

This directly matches the underlined portion's role—providing the cost-benefit justification for pursuing limited effects.

That confirms A is correct.
